result.title: What’s New in Google Accessibility | Episode 12 | American Sign Language
result.description:
Welcome back to another episode of What's New in Google Accessibility, a series where we share the latest product and feature updates that help make Google more accessible for everyone. In this episode, we'll tell you about updates on Android, Pixel, YouTube, Chrome Browser, and Gemini. 

Check out our full series at https://g.co/a11y/playlist 
To watch this video without American Sign Language (ASL) interpretation, please click here: https://youtu.be/s0M79zWRKps
To learn more, sign up for our accessibility newsletter at: https://g.co/a11y/news 

0:00 Introduction
0:25 Left and Right Ear Controls | Android  
1:01 Talkback Keyboard Shortcut | Android 
1:24 3-Button Navigation | Android 
1:55 Mouse Keys Feature | Android 
2:08 Voice Access | Android 
3:22 Guided Frame | Pixel 
4:31 Caret Browsing | Chrome 
5:20 Voice Boost | YouTube
5:45 Tips for the neurodivergent community| Gemini
7:11 Closing

#WhatsNewInGoogle #Accessibility #Google #a11y

result.title: Try on @nike City Edition jerseys with Search 🏀✨
result.description:
See the fit before you buy. Open Google, search for any team jersey, select the product, and tap “Try it on.”

(Visuals and morphing video created with #Veo.)

result.title: Search What You See: The Tech Behind The Magic | Made by Google Podcast S9E4
result.description:
You see an outfit you love, but it’s not just the jacket you want—it’s the entire vibe. What if you could search for it all at once? In this episode, host Rachid Finge sits down with Product Manager Harsh Kharbanda to explore the next generation of Circle to Search, now available on Pixel 10. Discover how new multi-object recognition allows you to search an entire scene, from deconstructing a full outfit with 'Find the Look' to identifying every fish in an aquarium. Learn how advanced AI is making it easier than ever to satisfy your curiosity and shop for the whole picture, not just a single piece.

01:28 – The Shift from Camera to Screen
07:18 – Introducing "Find the Look"
10:34 – The Virtual "Try it On" Tool
14:43 – Beyond Fashion: Skincare, Design, and Facts

#AI #CircleToSearch #MadeByGoogle #TechPodcast

result.title: Build Apps for Your Friends (Vibe Coding)
result.description:
In this video, we show you how you can surprise your community with personalized apps, cards, and interactive experiences built on Google AI Studio. First, we vibe code a custom card generator that we can tailor to different tastes and celebrations. Then, we use two shorter prompts to build interactive experiences that will surprise and delight our friends. Building with Gemini can be so fun! 
#VibeCoding #BuildWithGemini #AIStudio 

Here are the prompts for the second two builds: 
Fortune cookie card: Build an interactive, single-page digital birthday card using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that features a surprise 'fortune cookie' reveal. The initial view should display a large, illustrated fortune cookie resting on a festive background with a pulsing button that says 'Crack me open for your birthday fortune!' When the user clicks the cookie, trigger a smooth CSS animation that splits the cookie in half, revealing a small paper slip sliding out from the center. The text on the slip should be dynamically pulled from an array of fun, personalized birthday wishes. Once the paper is fully revealed, trigger a JavaScript confetti library to create a burst of celebratory confetti across the entire screen. Add a subtle sound effect when the cookie is clicked and another one for the confetti burst. 

Interactive garden card for mom: Build me a mother's day experience. She loves summer days and lilies. Make it interactive and add animations. Imagine a scene that is a never ending garden scene hand sketched on a summer day. And every corner there is a message for mom. She has to look around. Use the whole screen.

We have a lot of educational tutorials coming, so subscribe to this channel and keep an eye out for those! In the meantime, our “Build apps in AI Studio” article is a great resource for getting started: ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/aistudio-build-mode 

Build your ideas with Gemini: https://aistudio.google.com/apps
Info on how to ship your site or builds here: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/aistudio-build-mode#deploy-or
